briefing.headline: Slow and natural — summer stained water is prime soft plastic territory.
briefing.gameplan:
In summer with stained water and pre frontal conditions, soft plastics presentations shine.

Wheeler: In mornings bass sit looser to the outside of cover; as the sun gets up they move further into the cover, requiring a toad early and flipping deeper in the cover later.

Key adjustment for pre frontal: aggressive, faster retrieves — fish are feeding actively before the front.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: In mornings bass sit looser to the outside of cover; as the sun gets up they move further into the cover, requiring a toad early and flipping deeper in the cover later.
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on grass, ledge, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: An old-school TX-rigged 6-inch worm on ledges is something people forget about — you can go behind other anglers and get bites when fish won't eat a crankbait or are not grouped up. Use 3/8-o
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on grass, ledge, flat. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Palaniuk: 'I like to fish offshore and target shell bars or brushpiles with a 10-inch worm in summer.'
Wheeler: Best offshore spots included areas adjacent to grass lines, actual holes in the grass, and hard-bottomed shell beds in 8-35 feet of water.
Johnston: On the St. Lawrence, fish a mix of rock, sand, and weed in 15 feet or less. Hit as many spots as you can and hope the right ones bite.

briefing.depthStrategy: Pre-frontal summer bass feed aggressively. Fish split between shallow morning patterns (2-6ft) and offshore structure (12-25ft). Target grass lines, points, and ledges as falling pressure triggers movement.
adjustIf: If the front stalls and conditions stabilize, dial back aggression and focus on higher-percentage structure
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If bites dry up, try shaking in place for 10-15 seconds before moving — patience pays with plastics
